Label: AL rdfs:label@en
Definition: AL IAO:0000115@en

Label: environmental variable
Definition: A quality of an environmental system that reflects a physical, chemical, or hydrological condition of the abiotic environment and that can be measured or observed.

Label: climatic and meteorological variable
Definition: An environmental variable that describes the state of the atmosphere at or near a site over a given period, such as temperature, precipitation, humidity, or radiation.

Label: air temperature
Definition: The temperature of the ambient air at a specified height above the ground surface at a site.

Label: precipitation amount
Definition: The depth of water (from rain, snow, hail, or other forms) that reaches the ground surface at a site over a specified period.

Label: relative humidity
Definition: The ratio of the amount of water vapor actually present in the air to the amount that the air could hold at saturation at the same temperature, expressed as a percentage.

Label: wind speed
Definition: The rate of horizontal air movement at a specified height above the ground surface at a site.

Label: photosynthetically active radiation
Definition: The amount of solar radiation in the 400-700 nm waveband reaching a site, which is the spectral range used by plants for photosynthesis.
Comment: Often reported as an instantaneous flux density (micromol photons per square metre per second) or integrated as daily light integral.

Label: growing degree days
Definition: A cumulative measure of heat accumulation calculated from the amount by which daily mean air temperature exceeds a specified base temperature over a growing season, used to predict plant development timing.

Label: soil variable
Definition: An environmental variable that describes a physical, chemical, or biological property of the soil at a site.

Label: soil pH
Definition: A measure of the acidity or alkalinity of a soil, expressed as the negative logarithm of the hydrogen ion activity in a soil-water or soil-solution mixture.

Label: soil temperature
Definition: The temperature of the soil at a specified depth below the ground surface.

Label: soil penetration resistance
Definition: The force per unit area required to push a probe or cone into the soil to a specified depth, used as a field indicator of soil compaction.

Label: soil organic matter content
Definition: The proportion, by mass, of a soil sample composed of organic material derived from decomposed plant, animal, and microbial residues.

Label: soil nutrient concentration
Definition: The concentration of a plant-available nutrient in a soil sample.

Label: soil nitrogen concentration
Definition: The concentration of plant-available nitrogen (e.g. nitrate or ammonium) in a soil sample.

Label: soil phosphorus concentration
Definition: The concentration of plant-available phosphorus in a soil sample.

Label: soil potassium concentration
Definition: The concentration of plant-available potassium in a soil sample.

Label: hydrological and water quality variable
Definition: An environmental variable that describes a physical, chemical, or hydraulic property of a water body such as a stream, river, wetland, or lake.

Label: stream discharge
Definition: The volume of water passing through a stream cross-section per unit time.

Label: stream flow velocity
Definition: The speed at which water moves through a stream channel at a specified point.

Label: water depth
Definition: The vertical distance from the water surface to the bed of a water body at a specified point.
Comment: Also referred to as stage when measured relative to a fixed reference gauge over time.

Label: water temperature
Definition: The temperature of the water in a water body at a specified depth and location.

Label: water pH
Definition: A measure of the acidity or alkalinity of a water sample, expressed as the negative logarithm of the hydrogen ion activity.

Label: dissolved oxygen concentration
Definition: The concentration of oxygen gas dissolved in a water sample, typically expressed as milligrams per litre or percent saturation.

Label: turbidity
Definition: A measure of the cloudiness or haziness of water caused by suspended particulate matter, typically expressed in nephelometric turbidity units.

Label: salinity
Definition: A measure of the total concentration of dissolved salts in a water sample.

Label: electrical conductivity of water
Definition: A measure of the capacity of a water sample to conduct electrical current, used as an indicator of its total dissolved ion concentration.

Label: nutrient concentration in water
Definition: The concentration of a dissolved nutrient, such as nitrate or phosphate, in a water sample.

Label: ecological variable
Definition: A quality of an organism, population, community, or ecosystem that reflects a biotic structural, compositional, or functional characteristic and that can be measured or observed.

Label: vegetation structure variable
Definition: An ecological variable that describes the physical arrangement, density, or size of plant material at a site.

Label: canopy cover
Definition: The proportion of ground area at a site that is vertically overhung by the combined foliage and branches of the plant canopy.
Comment: Distinct from canopy closure, which measures the proportion of overhead sky hemisphere obstructed by canopy from a point on the ground.

Label: canopy height
Definition: The vertical distance from the ground surface to the top of the plant canopy at a site.

Label: leaf area index
Definition: The one-sided green leaf area per unit ground surface area, a dimensionless index of canopy leaf density.

Label: stem density
Definition: The number of woody plant stems per unit ground area at a site.

Label: diameter at breast height
Definition: The diameter of a tree trunk measured at a standard height above the ground, conventionally 1.3 m internationally or 1.37 m (4.5 ft) in North America.

Label: basal area
Definition: The cross-sectional area of a tree trunk at breast height, or the sum of such areas for all trees per unit ground area at a site.

Label: understory cover
Definition: The proportion of ground area at a site covered by vegetation growing beneath the main forest canopy layer.

Label: shrub cover
Definition: The proportion of ground area at a site covered by the combined foliage of shrub-form vegetation.

Label: herbaceous cover
Definition: The proportion of ground area at a site covered by the combined foliage of herbaceous (non-woody) vegetation.

Label: litter depth
Definition: The vertical depth of the layer of dead plant material accumulated on the ground surface at a site.

Label: coarse woody debris volume
Definition: The volume, per unit ground area, of downed dead wood at a site above a specified minimum diameter.

Label: biodiversity and community composition variable
Definition: An ecological variable that describes the number, identity, relative representation, or spatial arrangement of species or individuals within a community at a site.

Label: species richness
Definition: The number of distinct species recorded within a defined sampling area or unit.

Label: species diversity
Definition: The variety of species present within a community, considering both the number of species and the relative abundance of individuals among them.

Label: species evenness
Definition: A measure of how uniformly individuals are distributed among the species present in a community, independent of species richness.

Label: percent native species cover
Definition: The proportion of ground area at a site covered by vegetation of species native to the region.

Label: percent invasive species cover
Definition: The proportion of ground area at a site covered by vegetation of species classified as invasive or non-native.

Label: percent bare ground cover
Definition: The proportion of ground area at a site with no vegetation, litter, or rock cover.

Label: population density
Definition: The number of individuals of a given species per unit area or volume of habitat.

Label: relative species abundance
Definition: The proportion that a given species' individuals represent of the total number of individuals of all species recorded in a community.

Label: productivity and biogeochemical variable
Definition: An ecological variable that describes the accumulation, storage, or cycling of biomass, carbon, or other materials at a site.

Label: aboveground biomass
Definition: The mass of living plant material, per unit ground area, located above the soil surface at a site.

Label: belowground biomass
Definition: The mass of living plant material, per unit ground area, located below the soil surface at a site.

Label: net primary productivity
Definition: The rate at which plants at a site accumulate biomass through photosynthesis minus their own respiratory losses, over a specified period.

Label: carbon stock
Definition: The mass of carbon stored, per unit ground area, in a specified pool (such as living biomass, dead wood, litter, or soil) at a site.

Label: decomposition rate
Definition: The rate at which dead organic material (such as litter or coarse woody debris) breaks down and loses mass at a site.

Label: litterfall rate
Definition: The rate at which dead plant material (leaves, twigs, and other fine debris) is deposited onto the ground surface at a site.

Label: disturbance and pressure variable
Definition: An ecological variable that quantifies the frequency, intensity, or extent of a natural or anthropogenic disturbance acting on a site.

Label: fire return interval
Definition: The average length of time between successive fire events at a site.
Comment: Distinct from fire frequency, which counts the number of fire events per unit time; the two are inversely related.

Label: grazing intensity
Definition: A measure of the degree to which vegetation at a site is consumed or trampled by grazing animals over a specified period.

Label: flood frequency
Definition: The number of flood events of a specified magnitude occurring at a site over a specified period.

Label: herbivory pressure
Definition: A measure of the degree to which plant tissue at a site is consumed by herbivores over a specified period.

Label: population demographic rate variable
Definition: An ecological variable that quantifies the rate at which individuals in a population are added or lost over a specified period.

Label: survival rate
Definition: The proportion of individuals present at the start of a specified period that remain alive at the end of that period.

Label: recruitment rate
Definition: The rate at which new individuals (through birth, germination, or establishment) are added to a population over a specified period.

Label: mortality rate
Definition: The rate at which individuals in a population die over a specified period.

Label: plant recruitment variable
Definition: An ecological variable that describes the germination, emergence, establishment, or early growth of individual plants at a site.

Label: seedling survival rate
Definition: The proportion of seedlings present at the start of a specified monitoring period that remain alive at the end of that period.

Label: seedling establishment rate
Definition: The proportion of seedlings or introduced propagules that successfully transition from initial emergence to persistence as established plants at a restoration site over a specified period.

Label: seedling growth rate
Definition: The rate of increase in size (height, basal diameter, or dry mass) of individual seedlings over a specified time period at a restoration site.

Label: seedling emergence rate
Definition: The rate at which seeds germinate and produce seedlings that emerge above the soil surface at a site over a specified period.

Label: seedling height
Definition: The vertical distance from the base of a seedling at soil level to its apical meristem or tallest leaf tip at the time of measurement.

Label: seedling natural regeneration density
Definition: The number of naturally regenerating seedlings per unit ground area at a site, arising without direct planting or seeding by humans.

Label: height growth increment
Definition: The increase in height of a tree or seedling over a specified time period, used as a measure of vertical growth performance.

Label: root sucker density
Definition: The number of root suckers — shoots arising from adventitious buds on the lateral roots or root collar of a parent plant — per unit ground area at a site over a specified monitoring period.

Label: water table depth
Definition: The vertical distance from the ground surface to the upper boundary of the saturated zone in the soil or peat at a site.
Comment: Positive values indicate the water table is below ground surface; a value of zero indicates the water table is at the surface; negative values indicate inundation. Also reported as water table level relative to a fixed datum.

Label: light availability
Definition: The amount of photosynthetically active radiation reaching a specified position such as the forest floor or understory, typically expressed as a proportion of above-canopy incident radiation or as an absolute flux density.

Label: Sphagnum cover
Definition: The proportion of ground area at a peatland site covered by living Sphagnum mosses.
Comment: Sphagnum cover is the standard measured quantity at a point in time; the term 'Sphagnum regeneration' is commonly used in the peatland restoration literature to refer to the same phenomenon, particularly when framing it as a post-restoration outcome.

Label: microbial biomass
Definition: The mass of living microbial cells (bacteria, fungi, and other microorganisms) per unit mass or volume of soil at a site.

Label: vegetation cover
Definition: The proportion of ground area at a site covered by the combined foliage and stems of all vascular plant growth forms including trees, shrubs, and herbaceous plants.
Comment's database cross reference: Encompasses canopy cover (ELMO:3622302), shrub cover (ELMO:3622309), herbaceous cover (ELMO:3622310), and understory cover (ELMO:3622308) as narrower terms. Use this term when reporting total across-strata plant cover.

Label: competition intensity
Definition: A measure of the degree to which neighbouring plants reduce the growth, survival, or reproductive output of target plants at a site over a specified period.

Label: time since restoration
Definition: The elapsed time between the initiation of a restoration intervention at a site and the point of ecological measurement, used to characterize the site along a recovery trajectory.

Label: restoration success index
Definition: A composite measure evaluating the degree to which a restoration site has achieved specified ecological targets relative to a reference condition, assessed across one or more criteria such as species composition, structural complexity, or ecosystem function.

Label: ecological process variable
Definition: An ecological variable that describes the rate, stage, or trajectory of a natural ecological process occurring at a site.

Label: succession stage
Definition: A categorical or ordinal characterization of the stage of ecological succession at a site, based on the composition and structure of the plant community relative to a reference successional trajectory.

Label: natural regeneration rate
Definition: The rate at which native vegetation re-establishes at a site through unassisted ecological processes, without direct planting or seeding by humans, over a specified period.

Label: regeneration failure rate
Definition: The proportion of a site or planting cohort in which natural or assisted plant regeneration fails to achieve a specified establishment threshold over a monitoring period.

Label: habitat loss area
Definition: The area of habitat lost to anthropogenic disturbance, land-use change, or ecological degradation within a specified landscape unit over a defined time period.

Label: seismic line density
Definition: The total linear extent or area of seismic exploration corridors — linear clearings cut through forested terrain during geophysical surveys for oil and gas exploration — per unit landscape area at a site.
Comment: Seismic lines are a key disturbance type in Canadian boreal forest and peatland restoration, particularly in Alberta.
